Document [14] (Title: Corneal Topography -- Introduction) The Placido method analyzes only the anterior surface of the cornea, whereas the other two methods also map the posterior corneal surface and measure the corneal thickness. The Placido method involves projecting a series of concentric black and white rings onto the cornea and capturing their reflection by a camera placed in the center of the rings. This is the first Purkinje reflex. The power of the central 3 mm of the cornea is extrapolated, resulting in the Sim (simulated) K (keratometry) values. The rings are centered on the visual axis and not the pupil. The distance between the visual axis and the center of the pupil is the angle kappa (K). Algorithms calculate the power of the cornea at each point depending on the deformation of the mires. Document [23] (Title: Radial Keratotomy Correction -- Anatomy and Physiology) The eye acts as a compound optical system and comprises a corneal and a lens portion. The total refracting power of the normal eye is approximately 64 D, with the cornea accounting for two-thirds (43 D). The anterior and posterior corneal surfaces and the crystalline lens focus the incident light rays onto the retina. In an emmetropic (normally sighted) eye, light rays from infinity are focused on the retina with the dioptric power equal to the axial length of the eye. Images are therefore in focus. In ametropic eyes, the eye is either too long or short or has astigmatism, and thus the light rays are unable to be brought to a focus on the retina.

[31] Theoretical formulas are then used to calculate power. The largest contributor of error is the estimated lens position (ELP). Five generations of formulas have been developed to more accurately predict the lens position. The first generation formula, SRK, used a constant value for ELP; the second generation, Hoffer, formula used axial length to predict ELP; third-generation formulas (Holladay, SRK/T, and Hoffer Q) predict ELP using axial length and anterior corneal curvature; fourth and fifth-generation formulas use third-generation measurements and include anterior chamber depth. This classification helps clinicians understand the procedural context and approach for performing this particular type of lens extraction.
Document [26] (Title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 1 -- Community 156) الكاذب لمحفظة العدسة (pseudo‑exfoliation of the lens capsule). Surgical management includes procedures such as Extraction of after cataract with capsulotomy, which generates the child procedure إخراج العدسة (lens extraction); the procedure category “lens and cataract procedures” is parented by إخراج العدسة and targets the lens capsule as the direct procedural site. Extraction of primary membranous cataract by excision similarly leads to إخراج العدسة and is a member of اضطرابات العدسة. The one‑stage intracapsular extraction of the lens by inferior temporal route with insertion of a prosthetic intra‑ocular lens is defined as a lens and cataract procedure, underscoring the central role of lens capsule manipulation. This intra‑ocular procedure is performed within the eye, establishing a procedure_site_of relationship to the ocular structure, while the finding التقشّر الكاذب لمحفظة العدسة (pseudo‑erosion of the lens capsule) is localized to the lens capsule via a has_finding_site

Document [28] (Title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2622) The eye lens (oční čočka) is identified as the direct procedural site of lens extraction (إخراج العدسة). Lens extraction (إخراج العدسة) is also classified as a member of the lens disorders SMQ (اضطرابات العدسة (SMQ)), indicating that this surgical intervention is encompassed within the broader group of lens disorder concepts.
Document [29] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2690) Incision of tarsal gland is a specialized form of incision of skin, representing a more specific surgical action that targets the tarsal gland within the eyelid. Because it involves the eyelid structures, this procedure is also classified as one of the other therapeutic procedures on eyelids, conjunctiva, and cornea. Consequently, incision of tarsal gland bridges the general category of skin incisions with the broader group of ocular therapeutic interventions, highlighting its role in both dermatologic and ophthalmologic surgical care.
Document [30] (Title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1629) Ophthalmic Anti‑Infectives are categorized as a child class of Ophthalmic Agents, indicating that they are a specific type of medication used in the eye. As a subset of Ophthalmic Agents, Ophthalmic Anti‑Infectives are formulated to treat or prevent infections of the ocular surface and related structures, and they share the broader characteristics of agents applied to the eye for therapeutic purposes.
Document [31] (Title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 2 -- Community 842) Intracapsular extraction of the lens is a surgical technique classified under lens and cataract procedures. This operation can be further categorized by the surgical approach employed. A specific variant, the one‑stage intracapsular extraction of the lens by inferior temporal route and insertion of a prosthetic intraocular lens, is also considered a lens and cataract procedure. Recognizing these procedures as part of the broader lens and cataract surgery category helps clinicians select appropriate operative strategies and understand the role of approach selection and intraocular lens implantation in cataract management.
